rationale: The cited paper is thematically close to the citing paper’s stated gap: both examine how savings-goal framing/structure affects saving decisions, and the citing note explicitly says it extends prior earmarking/savings research into emergency-payment contexts. Given this direct conceptual overlap (not just a tangential topic match), the citation could plausibly be a substantive prior-theory/comparison reference and is not safe to drop as an obvious mention-only cite.
